title: Acceptable Aliases for Generic Procurement Contract MVP
description: Acceptable aliases for matching AI-generated ontology names to canonical gold ontology ids. This file is not a known conflicts file and not a scoring script.

aliases.note: Generic request only acceptable when procurement context is clear
Only acceptable in procurement order context
Potentially ambiguous; do not use for purchase request
Only acceptable when supplier invoice context is clear

non_equivalences.reason: PurchaseRequest is a pre-approval demand object; PurchaseOrder is an operational order released after contract activation. PR and PO must not be merged.
PurchaseRequestItem is a line record with quantity and price; Material is the requested good/service semantics represented by item description/category.
ApprovalTask records workflow decisions; Payment is a finance payment request/execution object. Payment approval records should not be merged into Payment itself.
Supplier/Vendor is external provider; Employee/User is internal actor. Do not merge even though both can have contact information.
Contract is legal agreement; PurchaseOrder is operational order against a contract.
GoodsReceipt/GRN is receiving evidence; Invoice is supplier billing document. They are matched but not equivalent.
Invoice is billing document; Payment/Payment Request is finance approval and execution for an invoice.
Department is organizational unit; Employee/User is an actor belonging to a department.
Agreement/Contract should not be confused with payment agreement or payment request.

usage_notes: Aliases are intended for name matching tolerance in future scoring.
An alias maps to exactly one canonical_id in this file.
Ambiguous generic words should only be accepted when surrounding context matches the canonical concept.
Non-equivalences define boundaries that must not be merged even when terms look similar.
